sutta: this is quite an important suta the buddha said monks there are these 4 ways of development of concentration samadhi bhavana what for there is monks the development of concentration which when developed and made much of conduces to happy living in this very life there is monks the development of concentration which when developed and made much of conduces to winning knowledge and insight nyana dasana there is monks the development of concentration which conduces to mindfulness and well awareness there is monks that which when developed and made much of conduces to the destruction of the asavas now monks what sort of development of concentration conduces to happy living in this very life herein among aloo from sen's desires etc attains the first jana by calming down of thought directed and sustained he attains the second jhana by the fading out of delight etc he attains the third jana by abandonment of pleasure and pain and lying etc he attains the fourth jana and abides therein this is called the development of concentration which conduces to happy living in this very life i'll just stop for a while to make a comment here out of the 4 developments of concentration the first 1 conduces to happy living to blissful living happy abiding is attaining the jaanas some of you might have heard a previous suta where the buddha talked about the different kinds of cultures that he can attain and he said that when he comes out of the fort janna if he walks he's walking in bliss if he if he lies down he's lying down is in bliss if he sits he's sitting in bliss if he's standing he's standing in bliss etc because when the person comes out of the fort janna the effect of the jana is still there he still feels a strong effect the bliss and the attenuation of the hindrances so makes him very very blissful so this is what the buddha calls in certain sutas the bliss of renunciation attaining the bliss of samadhi and then to continue the buddha said and of what song is that which conduces to winning knowledge and insight herein among pays attention to consciousness of light he concentrates on consciousness of daylight as by day so by night as by night so by day thus with wits on alert with wits unhampered he cultivates the mind to brilliance this monks is called the development of concentration which when developed and made much of conduces to winning knowledge and insight i'll stop here again to commend this 1 the buddha says the condition which conduces to winning knowledge and inside is to cultivate the mind until it becomes bright and how do you cultivate the mind until it becomes bright is only by attaining the janus because the janus means state of mental incandescence the mind becomes bright that's why people who attain ajana and dwell constantly in ghana when they are reborn into the jhana plane they are beings of light they radiate a lot of light because the mind is so strong it becomes bright and the mind normally is dark because of defilements and the defilements are because our energies go out through the sixth sense doors and our energies go out to the sixth sense doors our energy is scattered so there's 1 suta where the buddha said if the mind does not uh it's not scattered when when the mind is not scattered then the the brightness of the mind manifests the mind is is naturally bright it's only because uh it is defiled by the uh sensed doors the objects of the senses and that the mind becomes scattered and dull so here you see this is a very important passage here which is to say yeah that's why a lot of people don't understand what how come samadhi can give us knowledge and insight how can somebody by itself give us wisdom and this is the point here the buddha is trying to make that by cultivating our mind until it's so strong that it's so bright now that is the type of mind that is very sharp that can see very clearly they can see things as they really are because the buddha said elsewhere that the condition for young [Music] seeing things as they really are is uh samadhi the condition for seeing things as they really are is strong somebody so here uh especially if the mind is is bright now is is very very strong very very concentrated that is the best condition for nyanadasana attaining knowledge and insider now to continue on the buddha said and what sort of development of concentration conduces to mindfulness and well awareness satisfacts the feelings which arise in among are evident to him the feelings which abide with him are evident to him the feelings which come to an end in him are evident to him the perceptions sanya which arise in him are evident etc the trains of thought vitaka which arise which abide with him which come to an end in him are evident this monks is called the development of concentration which conduces to mindfulness and well awareness i'll stop here for a while
commentary: so here the buddha saying is saying if we uh want to be uh to have satisfaction mindful and clear comprehension or well awareness then we should be always looking to into our mind always watching these 3 things vedana the feelings sannya the perceptions and the thoughts we taka we keep on watching our mind that is conducive to mindfulness and well awareness so here you see here mindfulness and well awareness is the practice of watching the mind it is not the practice of seeing outside objects not to be aware of sights sounds smells etc but to be aware of our working of the mind because the problem is in our mind where does the ego arise ego arises in the mind so that is why we must always be watching the mind and then to continue and what sort of development of concentration if developed and made much of conducive to the destruction of the hearts us herein among dwells observing the rise and fall in the 5 aggregates of grasping thus such is body such is the arising of body such is its vanishing such is feeling such is perception such as volition [Music] uh the arising of volition the vanishing of religious volitions such as consciousness such the arising of consciousness such the vanishing of consciousness this monks is called the development of concentration which conduces to the this to the destruction of the asavas these are the 4 forms of it so the last 1 if you want to destroy the us now we should always look into the 5 aggregates and what are these 5 aggregates body first 1 second 1 is feeling perception volition and consciousness and these 5 things now if you break them down it's basically 2 things the first 1 is body and the other 4 is mind the active mind the working mind so if we want to uh destroy the asavas to attain we look into our body and mind always be observant of our body and mind and why do why basically do you want to observe the body and mind because the buddha said that if we have any idea about the self the clinging to the self we this self that we cling to what do we associate with it's always with these 2 things body and mind we always associate with the self with the body and mind so that's why we should be always observing the nature of our body how our dog body depends on food our body depends on the conditions if the weather is too hot make it sick but it's too cold we can't stand um if we don't get food we feel we feel sick and so the body needs to be sustained by food by good conditions so in the same way we watch the other factors of our mind how feeling comes arises through contact when there is contact through the sixth sense doors feeling arises and how it disappears also and similarly with perception also it needs contact at the sixth sense doors then only a feeling arises followed by perception and then volition the same way because of contact there's feeling there's perception and then there is thinking and then you decide what to do so all these are conditioned and same with consciousness consciousness arises because of our sense object and a sense uh our sense organ and the sense object which arises at our sense organs which makes the consciousness arise the seeing consciousness hearing consciousness etc so all these depend on conditions so we see how they arise and pass away arise and pass away how they are so entirely dependent on conditions slowly and we try to dissociate ourselves from these uh 5 things which are basically body and mind so this is uh this is a suta which talks about samadhi bhavana development of samadhi concentration the different types of samadhi bhavana first 1 conduces to happy living second 1 conduces to winning knowledge and insight and this 1 is very important how we must develop our mind is clear and then we see things very we have a very alert mind not a no not the mind that is hampered by the 5 hindrances if our mind is not developed if we don't have samadhi it's always enveloped by the 5 hindrances which uh block us which hinder us from seeing things as they really are we see things as they seem to be just like a person with dark specks when you when you wear dark specks whatever you see here the color you see and it's always a perversion it's not the real real thing but you will swear you know but that is what you see that is only what it seems to you it's only when you remove the dark specks dark glasses that you see things in the correct perspective similarly we have the 5 hindrances now we see things in not as they really are but as they seem to be to us then the third 1 is the samani bhavana that conduces to mindfulness and well awareness and that is too the practice of it is to watch the mind and the last 1 conducing to the destruction of the asavas is to watch the 5 aggregates how they arise they pass away how they depend on conditions so that is quite an important suta for people who are interested in meditation that is why it is very important to study the sutas to investigate the sutas if you want to meditate in the right way because all the buddha's instructions are in the sutas including instructions on meditation now the next suta is
